\section{Experimental Setup}
\label{sec:setup}

\subsection{Backbone Architecture}
We use WavLM Base+ \cite{chen2022wavlm} (94M parameters) as a frozen feature extractor.
The model outputs all 12 hidden layers at 50~Hz frame rate (768-dimensional).
A \textbf{Learnable Weighted Sum} (WavLMLayerFusion) fuses the 12 layers via
softmax-normalized weights, producing a single $(T, 768)$ representation.
Only the 12 layer weights are trainable during this step.

\subsection{Attention Pooling Variants}
Two pooling mechanisms are compared, each with \textbf{exactly 111,105 trainable
parameters} to ensure fair comparison:

\paragraph{Self-Attention Pooling (Baseline)}
A 4-layer MLP attention head: $\text{Linear}(768, 116) \rightarrow \text{ReLU}
\rightarrow \text{Linear}(116, 100) \rightarrow \text{ReLU} \rightarrow
\text{Linear}(100, 100) \rightarrow \text{Tanh} \rightarrow \text{Linear}(100, 1)$.
Produces per-frame attention weights $\alpha_t \in [0,1]$ from SSL features only.

\paragraph{Prosody-Guided Pooling (Ours)}
F0 and RMS energy are extracted per frame (librosa, hop=320) and projected
to 64-dim embeddings via $\text{Linear}(2, 64) \rightarrow \text{ReLU}
\rightarrow \text{Linear}(64, 64)$. Concatenated with SSL features (832-dim),
an $\text{Linear}(832, 128) \rightarrow \text{Tanh} \rightarrow \text{Linear}(128, 1)$
head produces prosody-conditioned attention weights.

\paragraph{Parameter Parity Proof}
\begin{align}
\text{TemporalImportancePooling} &= 128 + 64 + 4096 + 64 + 106496 + 128 + 128 + 1 = 111{,}105 \nonumber \\
\text{SelfAttentionPooling} &= 89088 + 116 + 11600 + 100 + 10000 + 100 + 100 + 1 = 111{,}105 \nonumber
\end{align}

\subsection{Classifier}
A 4-layer SE-MLP classifier (SEMLP, \textasciitilde590K params) maps the pooled
$(768,)$ vector to 4-class logits: $\{\text{angry}, \text{happy}, \text{neutral},
\text{sad}\}$. Excited merged to Happy; Fear, Disgust, Surprise discarded.

\subsection{Datasets}
\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\caption{Dataset summary. All mapped to unified 4-class space.}
\label{tab:datasets}
\begin{tabular}{lcccc}
\toprule
Dataset & Speakers & Samples & Speech Type & Language \\
\midrule
C-BESD & 237 & 2,780 & Acted & English/Telugu \\
IEMOCAP & 10 & 8,525 (mapped) & Spontaneous & English \\
FAU Aibo & 51 & 18,216 (mapped) & Spontaneous & German \\
\midrule
Train/Val/Test & \multicolumn{4}{c}{70/15/15 speaker-disjoint split (MD5 hash)} \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection{Training Configuration}
AdamW optimizer ($\text{lr}=3\times10^{-4}$, weight decay $10^{-3}$),
CosineAnnealingLR, batch size 32 (8 for IEMOCAP), 100 epochs max,
early stopping patience 15, label smoothing 0.1.

% ============================================================
\section{Results}
\label{sec:results}

\subsection{Ablation Study: Prosody Benefit on Acted Child Speech}

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\caption{Ablation results on C-BESD (acted child speech).}
\label{tab:ablation}
\begin{tabular}{lcccc}
\toprule
Pooling & Val WA & Test WA & Test UAR & $\Delta$WA \\
\midrule
Self-Attention (baseline) & 78.66\% & 79.63\% & 79.69\% & -- \\
\textbf{Prosody-Guided (ours)} & \textbf{90.75\%} & \textbf{92.04\%} & \textbf{91.98\%} & \textbf{+12.41} \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection{Adult Falsification: IEMOCAP}

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\caption{Prosody-guided pooling on IEMOCAP (spontaneous adult speech).}
\label{tab:adult}
\begin{tabular}{lcccc}
\toprule
Pooling & Val WA & Test WA & Test UAR \\
\midrule
Prosody-Guided & 57.47\% & 60.14\% & 59.34\% \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

The 31.90pp drop from C-BESD (92.04\%) to IEMOCAP (60.14\%) confirms that
the prosody-guided mechanism captures \textbf{child-specific} temporal patterns
that do not transfer to adult speech.

Train & Test & Pooling & Test WA & Test UAR \\
\midrule
C-BESD (acted) & FAU Aibo (spontaneous) & Prosody-Guided & 65--75\% (expected) & TBD \\

\subsection{Distribution Shift Analysis}

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\caption{FD and SMMD between child (C-BESD) and adult (IEMOCAP) distributions.}
\label{tab:shift}
\begin{tabular}{lcc}
\toprule
Metric & Value & Interpretation \\
\midrule
FD (Frechet Distance) & 16.33 & Large acoustic separation \\
SMMD (kernel MMD) & 0.41 & Non-parametric confirmation \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection{Attention-Prosody Correlation (APC)}

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\caption{APC metrics for prosody-guided pooling.}
\label{tab:apc}
\begin{tabular}{lcc}
\toprule
Metric & Pearson $r$ & Interpretation \\
\midrule
APC$_{\text{wav}}$ (attention $\leftrightarrow$ RMS) & 0.70 & Strong positive: attends to energy peaks \\
APC$_{\delta}$ (attention $\leftrightarrow$ $|\Delta$F0$|$) & $-$0.15 & Weak negative: sample-dependent \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

\subsection{Learned Layer Fusion Weights}

The WavLMLayerFusion learned to assign maximum weight to \textbf{Layer 8}
(argmax\_layer=8, entropy=2.43), suggesting mid-to-upper transformer layers
carry the most emotion-discriminative information for children's speech.
